LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MICHAEL TEJADA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO SEPTEMBER 13, 2013 TO GIVE STATE FARM THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B, NO LATER THAN AUGUST 23, 2013 BY 4:00 P.M. MR. TEJADA DELIVERED COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPERATIVE COMPLAINT TO DEPARTMENT 302 WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE AT THE HEARING. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING AND REPLY PAPERS. IF STATE FARM FAILS TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6(B), THE COURT WOULD PROCEED WITH THE HEARING ON SEPTEMBER 13, 2013. MR. SHAHINHAH TO INFORM OPPOSING COUNSEL OF THE NEW HEARING DATE. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MICHAEL TEJADA'S DEMURRER TO STATE FARM'S COMPLAINT AND CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED IN ITS ENTIRETY. THE COMPLAINT STATES FACTS SUFFICIENT TO STATE A PROPERTY DAMAGES CLAIM UNDER EQUITABLE SUBROGATION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM
LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MICHAEL TEJADA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR; DUPLICATE ENTRY.

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT TOMASICO YAMBAO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. TAKING THE FACTS IN THE COMPLAINT AS TRUE, PLAINTIFF HAS PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO CONSTITUTE A CLAIM OF EQUITABLE SUBROGATION. (SEE FIREMAN'S FUND INS. CO. V. MARYLAND CAS. CO. (1998) 65 CAL. APP. 4TH 1279, 1292.) DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER THAT THE COMPLAINT IS UNCERTAIN IS OVERRULED. TAKING THE FACTS AS TRUE, DEFENDANT YAMBAO CAN REASONABLY DETERMINE WHAT ISSUES MUST BE ADMITTED OR DENIED, OR WHAT COUNTS AND CLAIMS ARE DIRECTED AGAINST HIM OR HER. (SEE KHOURY V. MALY'S OF CALIFORNIA INC. (1993) 14 CAL.APP.4TH 612, 616.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RICHARD KRAMER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT MICHAEL TEJADA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT- THE HEARING IS CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO SEPTEMBER 20, 2013 AT 9:30 A.M. THERE APPEARS TO BE CONFUSION AS TO THE HEARING DATE. SOME PAPERS IDENTIFY SEPTEMBER 13 AS THE HEARING DATE AND OTHERS IDENTIFY SEPTEMBER 23. THE COURT DOES NOT HAVE A COURTESY COPY OF THE NOTICE OF NEW HEARING DATE FOR DEMURRER FILED BY DEFENDANT ON SEPTEMBER 3, 2013, AND THIS DOCUMENT IS NOT SCANNED ON THE COURT'S COMPUTERIZED REGISTER OF ACTIONS. THE COURT NOW HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE PLEADING AT ISSUE AND THE MOVING AND OPPOSING PAPERS ON DEMURRER. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL IS REQUESTED TO PROVIDE A COURTESY COPY OF THE NOTICE OF NEW HEARING DATE THAT IT FILED ON SEPTEMBER 3, 2013 TO DEPARTMENT 302. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL TO GIVE NOTICE TO PLAINTIFF OF THE NEW HEARING DATE. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-13-2013 Continued To Sep-20-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ELIZABETH PACHECO, TEL. NO. (707) 733-8282 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
ATTY. NIMA SHAAHINFAR, TEL. NO. (310) 804-1915 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT MICHAEL TEJADA's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT- The hearing is continued on the Court's own motion to September 20, 2013 at 9:30 a.m. There appears to be confusion as to the hearing date. Some papers identify September 13 as the hearing date and others identify September 23. The Court does not have a courtesy copy of the Notice of New Hearing Date for Demurrer filed by Defendant on September 3, 2013, and this document is not scanned on the Court's computerized register of actions. The Court now has courtesy copies of the pleading at issue and the moving and opposing papers on demurrer. Defendant's counsel is requested to provide a courtesy copy of the Notice of New Hearing Date that it filed on September 3, 2013 to Department 302. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL TO GIVE NOTICE TO PLAINTIFF OF THE NEW HEARING DATE.
Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM

MINI MINUTES:

RULING - DEFENDANT MICHAEL TEJADA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is continued to September 13, 2013 to give State Farm the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B, no later than August 23, 2013 by 4:00 p.m. Mr. Tejada delivered courtesy copies of the operative Complaint to Department 302 with a cover letter stating the new hearing date at the hearing. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the moving and reply papers. If State Farm fails to comply with Local Rule 2.6(B), the court would proceed with the hearing on September 13, 2013. Mr. Shahinhah to inform opposing counsel of the new hearing date. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

Off Calendar May-21-2013 Continued To Jul-17-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-17-2013 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JOSEPH NYKODYM, TEL. NO. (925) 262-7045 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT TOMASICO YAMBAO's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled. Taking the facts in the Complaint as true, Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to constitute a claim of equitable subrogation. (See Fireman's Fund Ins. Co. v. Maryland Cas. Co. (1998) 65 Cal. App. 4th 1279, 1292.) Defendant's demurrer that the Complaint is uncertain is overruled. Taking the facts as true, Defendant Yambao can reasonably determine what issues must be admitted or denied, or what counts and claims are directed against him or her. (See Khoury v. Maly's of California Inc. (1993) 14 Cal.App.4th 612, 616.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Richard Kramer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/RAK)
